name: Land of the Lady Loop
name_original: A la rencontre de la Dame de Brassempouy — boucle au départ d'Amou
description: Cycle the challenging 38.6-mile Meeting the Lady of Brassempouy loop from Amou, exploring historical sites and varied terrain.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Amou traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features a mix of agricultural lands and forested sections, providing scenic backdrops for rides. Elevation changes are present, ranging from gentle inclines to more sustained climbs. The area's network of roads and paths supports a range of cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Amou The most popular touring cycling route is Land of the Lady Loop, a 38.6 miles (62.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Pont Vieux of Orthez – Lakes of the Barthes de Biron loop from Parouilles, a moderate 22.9 miles (36.9 km) path. This route offers views of the Pont Vieux and passes by the Lakes of the Barthes de Biron. Local touring cyclists also love the Pierre de Baisha-cuu loop from Rontun, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 42 minutes. Touring cycling around Amou is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Amou are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 180 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Amou's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Amou?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Amou, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored more than 180 of these routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring bike routes in Amou?
A: Touring cycling routes around Amou feature a diverse landscape of rolling hills and river valleys. You'll encounter a mix of agricultural lands and forested sections, with elevation changes ranging from gentle inclines to more sustained climbs.

Q: Are there any easy touring bike routes suitable for beginners or families in Amou?
A: Yes, Amou offers routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35661696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pierre de Baisha-cuu loop from Rontun&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.8-mile (9.3 km) trail that takes about 42 minutes to complete, leading through pleasant rural landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging touring bike routes for experienced cyclists in Amou?
A: Experienced cyclists looking for a challenge can tackle routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1286956786/land-of-the-lady-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Land of the Lady Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 38.6-mile (62.1 km) trail features significant elevation gain and varied terrain, typically taking around 3 hours 54 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any circular touring bike routes around Amou?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Amou are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35737979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Amou&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.1-mile (22.8 km) option that offers a convenient circular journey.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Amou?
A: Along the routes, you can discover various points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41602239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont Vieux of Orthez – Lakes of the Barthes de Biron loop from Parouilles&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers views of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3237416" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont Vieux of Orthez&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/811823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Peter's Church&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6824821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncade Castle - Orthez&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Amou?
A: The touring cycling routes in Amou are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain and scenic backdrops provided by the region's rolling hills and river valleys.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Amou?
A: The region around Amou generally offers pleasant cycling conditions from spring through autumn. Spring brings blooming landscapes, while autumn provides cooler temperatures and beautiful foliage, making both seasons ideal for exploring the varied terrain.

Q: Are there any routes that follow dedicated cycle paths or greenways?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate sections of dedicated paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20638779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Voie verte de Chalosse – Mugron area loop from Bergouey&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 19.8-mile (31.9 km) route that utilizes parts of the Voie verte de Chalosse, offering a smoother cycling experience.

Q: What should I consider regarding parking when planning a touring bike trip in Amou?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead isn't always detailed, routes often start from towns or villages like Amou or Parouilles, where public parking options are typically available. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

Q: Are there any routes that offer good viewpoints or scenic overlooks?
A: Given Amou's landscape of rolling hills and river valleys, many routes naturally offer scenic views of the surrounding agricultural lands and forested areas. The varied elevation changes provide opportunities for panoramic vistas, especially from higher points along the trails.
tips: Gaujacq Castle is an unusual masterpiece built in the image of an Italian building, with its botanical garden with 3,500 plant species.

Romanesque building with circular apse and rectangular bell tower, of which only the apse remains, dating from the end of the 12th century. Inside, you can see 18th century woodwork, a monumental 17th century wooden chandelier, funerary slabs, and above all a marble high altar made around 1770 by the Mazzetti sculptors of Swiss origin who worked also in Laurède, Montaut, Aire sur l'Adour, Brocas les Forges, Mont de Marsan, Saint Pierre du Mont, Dax, Orthevielle, Pouillon and Sorde l'Abbaye.
Here the famous Lady of Brassempouy was discovered in 1894, today attested as the first human face.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Meeting the Lady of Brassempouy — loop from Amou?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring bicycle route is estimated to take approximately 3 hours and 55 minutes to complete. However, this can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the sights.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route predominantly features paved surfaces, with about 52.7 km of asphalt and 9.34 km of other paved roads. Be aware that some segments may be unpaved and difficult to navigate, potentially requiring you to push your bike. It's designed for cyclists with very good fitness levels.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Meeting the Lady of Brassempouy loop?
faqs.answer: This loop offers a journey through cultural and historical landmarks. Highlights include the architectural masterpiece of Gaujacq Castle with its botanical garden, the Romanesque Saint-Martin Church of Caupenne, and the significant Brassempouy Prehistoric Site, where the famous 'Lady of Brassempouy' was discovered.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, Amou serves as the starting point for this loop. You would typically look for public parking options within Amou to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and is designed for those with very good fitness levels. With 62.1 kilometers and an elevation gain of 680 meters, it is not recommended for beginners or young families looking for an easy ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route would generally be during spring or autumn when the weather is milder and more predictable. Summer can be pleasant but may involve hotter temperatures, while winter might bring colder conditions and potentially less favorable road surfaces.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a touring bicycle route, dogs are generally not suitable companions unless they are specifically trained for cycling alongside or carried in a suitable trailer. Always ensure local regulations regarding dogs in public areas are followed.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there is no specific permit required or entrance fee to cycle the 'Meeting the Lady of Brassempouy — loop from Amou' route. It utilizes public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other local trails. You will find it intersects with parts of the Circuit entre Louts et Voie Verte and the Tour de la Haute Chalosse.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or accommodation options along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages, including Amou and Brassempouy, where you may find local cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options. It's advisable to check opening hours and availability in advance, especially in smaller communities.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given its length and difficulty, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling gear, and navigation tools. Sun protection and layers of clothing are also recommended, depending on the season.
